content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as layered, self-curating collective agents where individual memory entries act as autonomous curiosity-driven sub-agents that compete for retrieval based on how much new information they can add to the agent’s current query, rather than just being static stored tokens. This sub-agent memory architecture naturally amplifies the active learning objective by prioritizing memory entries that reduce uncertainty over the current task, outperforming traditional retrieval-based memory organized only by similarity or recency.
